hypothesis: If a JAX-based MLIP library implements optimized architectures (including a gated, species-dependent MACE) and integrates with JAX-MD/ASE, then it will yield faster inference and MD simulation runtimes on H100 GPUs while maintaining comparable energy and force prediction accuracy to established PyTorch implementations of MACE, NequIP, and ViSNet because JAX compilation and the architectural modifications reduce computational overhead.
